title: Application control allowlist drift monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'ASD Essential Eight' → 'E8.01'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: Windows Security Event Logs, endpoint protection logs, cloud audit trails, vulnerability scan outputs, email security logs, network infrastructure logs, identity management systems, incident response records
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4688 earliest=-24h
| stats count by New_Process_Name, host
| lookup asd_e8_scope.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true" AND NOT match(New_Process_Name,"(?i)\\\\Program Files")
value: Evidences ASD Essential Eight obligations for application control allowlist drift monitoring using endpoint, identity, and cloud telemetry.
implementation: (1) Ingest CrowdStrike policy posture via TA 5082 and map hosts to NDB and NZISM tiers. (2) Tune alerts with ACSD Essential Eight and APRA-aligned severities. (3) Coordinate privacy and security ownership for dual-reporting workflows. (4) Monthly export for privacy officer and prudential working groups.
visualization: Single value (% compliant hosts), Bar chart (fails by control), Table (worst hosts).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
